metadata.title:
VAST SCOPE OF THE WORK
UNDERTAKEN BY THE


GOVERNMENT.

pages.raw_text:
WASHINGTON, d. c., April 1&-
Many persons will be surprised to
learn that before the government
turned stone an area equal to the
state of Massachusetts had, by pri-
vate irrigation enterprises, been
wrested from the unwatered and
valueless wastes of the west. Now
comes the United States govern
ment to lend hand and push for
ward the task of retaining the
desert places.


The secretary Of the interior es-
Inmates the reclaimable area of the
Great American Desert at 100000
acres, of which area the present
projects involve less than 1-25th. 1t
is the guess of experts that the
government will soon be retaining
the desert at the rate Of 1,000,0OO
| acres a year. At the end of the
year 1925, for instance, not less
than 100000 acres will certainly
have been reclaimed and put under
cultivation. The money spent in


retaining land must, under the
law, be repaid by settlers in annual
payments extending over a period
